title: libpod and varlink version (ctx context)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I generate the Go Bindings for the Podman Varlink API, it uses methods from the `varlink.Connection` yet it does not provide the first argument that is required, which should be `ctx  context`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

Running on Fedora Server 31

comments.body: Is there a reason you want to do this with Varlink, versus APIV2?
@rhatdan dont assign this to me! you already stated you didn't want this fixed when Jhon asked you about it.  Are you changing your mind?  Harald changed the the varlink api which broke us

Don't recall.  Anyways just looking for your comment.
@bruno-anjos We are deprecating varlink support.  We would love to work with you on APIV2 support.

There is no reason for me to use the old API versus the new one. I just read the New API announcement in the blog, and I thought the varlink API was still the one fully implemented and working, therefore the one I should be using.

Is the new APIV2 already in a usable state? I would also be using Go, so the bindings mentioned in the post would also be really useful. Is there anything you can point me to to follow the development state of the new APIV2 (besides the blog and GitHub)?

Side note I also thought that the whole varlink API was a bit confusing, so I believe that the change to the new API will be well received by the community.

I would gladly help you guys testing APIV2. Just to give some context I would be using the Podman API mainly to do container migration for my master thesis.

Well it is under heavy development, but for most use cases it is probably ready.  @baude could give you the most up2date information, and the documentation on it is pretty good.

Any help you can give us on testing would be greatly appreciated.
